title: WFS into PostgreSQL: Lakes in Italy
text:
Install ogr_fdw
The tool ogr_fdw

CREATE SERVER geoserver_ita
    FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER ogr_fdw
    OPTIONS (
        datasource 'WFS:http://www.ideandalucia.es/services/DERA_g11_patrimonio/wfs',
        format 'WFS' );

title: WFS into PostgreSQL: Lakes in Italy
text:
Create the table
Before filling with any data, PostgreSQL needed the columns names from the WFS and their data type. How do we know the name of the columns and their type if we don’t know yet even the name of the table. What does that WFS really contains? ogrinfo knows , type in your local terminal:

ogrinfo WFS:"http://www.ideandalucia.es/services/DERA_g11_patrimonio/wfs" -summary

Focus on ‘1: ID.ACQUEFISICHE.SPECCHI.ACQUA’ . This is the name of the only table this WFS has. Now that we know the name of the table, let’s see the ogrinfo of that tables. There are 2 options: a) Use “-so” to observe only the column names, otherwise it will read all the data, which in this case takes a while.

ogrinfo -so WFS:"http://www.ideandalucia.es/services/DERA_g11_patrimonio/wfs" ID.ACQUEFISICHE.SPECCHI.ACQUA

Instead of typing everything, copy the content and ask ChatGPT “create a postgresql using the following information”<paste the summary from the code 1>“. To use the data from the WFS, include SERVER and OPTION.

CREATE FOREIGN TABLE pgrouting_book.specchi_acqua_ita(
    fid BIGINT, -- Field for the unique identifier
    boundary GEOMETRY, -- Assumed to be a spatial data type, change as necessary
    gml_id VARCHAR(255), -- GML identifier
    nome VARCHAR(255), -- Name of the water body
    origine VARCHAR(255), -- Origin of the data
    descrizione_origine VARCHAR(255), -- Description of the origin
    nasp VARCHAR(255), -- Additional information
    natura_specchio_acqua VARCHAR(255) -- Nature of the water body
)
SERVER "geoserver_ita"
    OPTIONS (layer 'ID.ACQUEFISICHE.SPECCHI.ACQUA');

To validate the data, execute the following code that will return the column and the data type that they contain.

SELECT  column_name, data_type
FROM information_schema.columns
WHERE table_name = 'id_acquefisiche_specchi_acqua';


WITH area AS (
  SELECT 
    fid,
    st_area(ST_CurveToLine(boundary)::geography)/10000 as area_hm2
  FROM 
    pgrouting_book.specchi_acqua_ita
)
SELECT 
  acqua_ita.*, 
  area.area_hm2
FROM 
  pgrouting_book.specchi_acqua_ita AS acqua_ita
JOIN 
  area ON acqua_ita.fid = area.fid 
WHERE 
  acqua_ita.natura_specchio_acqua IN ('Lago', 'Bacino artificiale')
  AND area.area_hm2 > 0.2 
LIMIT 5;

title: Ricardo Ruiz Sánchez
text:
Data-driven environmental scientist with a passion for geographical information data. Started collecting data from the laboratory as a technician, I graduated in Environmental Sciences understanding the value of making informed-decisions towards a sustainable future. Along this journey I lived in Scotland and Germany.
Drafted by the interest on geospatial data I am in my second year of a master in Geographical Information Management and Applications (GIMA).

title: Ricardo Ruiz Sánchez
text:
Education
 Msc in Geographical Information Management and Applications
 Bsc in Environmental Sciences
 Higher vocational training as Laboratory technician in quality control

title: Ricardo Ruiz Sánchez
text:
Languages
 English (C1, advance)
 German (B1, intermediate)
 Spanish (Native)

title: Internship
text:
I am excited about the opportunity to intern in a Geo-Information workplace to apply my practical and theoretical skills from the master GIMA, Geographical Information Management and Applications1.This internship is also an opportunity for the organizations interested. The four different universities that co-operates in this GIMA master offer a diversity in fields and their own traditions, so you can choose which one better suit your needs to carry out a research during the internship.



Four universities in the Netherlands take part in the GIMA Master, which stands for Geographical Information Management and Applications


Your organization can incorporate a research component from geo-information experts of these universities. Additionally, the internship also allocates time for the intern to gain professional skills helping you with existing projects, working on programming skills or project management tasks.The These are the focus of the Universities in GIMA:

Utrecht University: Geography and planning.
Delft University of Technology: Legal, organisational and technical aspects of geo-information handling.
Wageningen University: Agricultural and rural applications.
University of Twente: Technical and application oriented courses for developing countries.

If you are interested, he next sections give more information about this internship to better understand the process.
To accommodate organizations and students needs, GIMA offers three different options to arrange an Internship. The option A is focused on one company, while option B lasts the half and includes two companies. Lastly, option C is an intermediate with 540 hours as internship and 280 h to do 10 ECTs in courses. In all cases, 60% of the duration is spent to research a topic and 40% to gain professional skills, for example helping with existing projects.
The table 1 summarize these options and Figure 1 illustrates them.



Table 1: The duration and number of organizations varies between the different options of internship


Option
Organization
Duration
Hours




A
1 organization
5-6 months
840 h


B
2 organizations
2.5-3 months
420 h*2


C
1 organization and courses
3months
560 h+280 h











This GIMA internship is academic, which means that there is a research topic. Therefore, the requirements regarding the hours allocation are:


Time spent on research topic to be at least 60% of internship duration
Time spent on “work” is used to gain professional skills, e.g.:

internal courses
project management
programming skills
extra help with existing projects



That is why one of the possible deliverables at the end of the internship is an internship report (or article). This article can be either submitted to a peer reviewed jorunal or a professional GI related magazine.
More information about the GIMA internship can be found on page 44 of 82 in the GIMA Course Catalogue 2023-2024.
